title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples
text:
Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples - SupportGoWhere
Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples
Share link
Provides various stamp duty remissions for eligible couples buying new residential property or transferring their current one(s) as part of matrimonial proceedings.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Full Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) remission for couple’s joint purchase of first residential property
ABSD remission for a couple’s jointly purchased second property
Receive Buyer’s Stamp Duty (BSD), ABSD and Seller’s Stamp Duty (SSD) remissions when transferring property due to matrimonial proceedings
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

For married couples:

Full ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of their first residential property

Partial ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of a second residential property



For couples undergoing matrimonial proceedings:

Potential remission of BSD, ABSD and SSD on property transfers resulting from matrimonial proceedings

Who is this for?

Please refer to the following table for the various conditions and scenarios where a couple may receive remissions:



Joint purchase of first residential property by married couple

	

While ABSD will be applicable upon stamping, you may apply for the ABSD remission instead if you meet these conditions:

Married couple with at least 1 Singapore Citizen spouse

Property must be jointly purchased under couple’s names only

Both spouses do not already own any residential properties at the time of purchase






Joint purchase of second residential property by married couple

	

ABSD has already been paid on the second property

Married couple with at least 1 Singapore Citizen spouse

Property must be jointly purchased under couple’s names only

Couple must not own or have an interest in more than 1 residential property each when purchasing second property

Couple’s first property must be sold within 6 months after either:

Date of purchase of the second property (for completed property)

Issue date of the Temporary Occupation Permit (TOP) / Certificate of Statutory Completion (CSC), whichever is earlier, if the property was uncompleted at the time of purchase






Couples undergoing matrimonial proceedings

	

Transfer of property is due to either:

Court-ordered division of matrimonial assets (e.g. a proceeding for a decree of divorce, judicial separation or nullity of marriage under the law or religion)

Result of divorce proceedings

Property must be transferred from 1 party to another (both involved in the matrimonial proceedings)

Property may also be transferred by any child or children of the marriage / former marriage of the parties

How to apply?

For partial ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of a second residential property:

Navigate e-Stamping Portal to "Request" > "Apply for Refund"

For “Sale & Purchase – Purchase/ Acquisition” forms submitted on or after 2 Jul 2023, ABSD will be automatically refunded to the purchasers, within 6 weeks from the stamping of the first property’s sale, without needing a refund application

For stampings before 2 Jul 2023, you must manually provide the Stamp Duties (Spouses) (Remission of ABSD) refund application via the e-Stamping Portal > "Request" > "Apply for Refund"



For full ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of their first residential property:

e-Stamp via the e-Stamping Portal to obtain the remission certificate

Alternatively, a legal representative, where engaged, will assist with the stamping

Where can I find help?

For Remission of ABSD for a Married Couple: View more details 
For Matrimonial Proceedings: View more details

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Scheme last updated 12 Sep 2025
